periods.title: Key West waterfront and city grid in 1889
periods.summary: The first online Sanborn edition for Key West contains 16 sheets. This representative sheet is delivered directly from Wikimedia's image CDN, avoiding the page-level redirect that previously failed in MapLibre.
highlights: The Library of Congress dates this edition to April 1889 and provides all 16 sheets in the public domain.
The displayed sheet can be explored against the modern street grid and waterfront; the complete edition remains linked in the source catalog.
This initial rectangular placement is adjustable and should not be used for parcel or shoreline measurement.

overlay.title: 1889 Key West Sanborn sheet 4 — experimental fit
overlay.attribution: Sanborn Map Company / Library of Congress, April 1889; public domain

periods.title: Key West urban and waterfront expansion in 1892
periods.summary: The April 1892 Sanborn edition expands to 23 sheets and provides a second close-spaced record of the city and working waterfront before the Overseas Railway project.
highlights: The 1892 edition can be compared directly with 1889 to identify new blocks, industrial uses, and waterfront structures.
The displayed public-domain scan is requested directly from Wikimedia's image CDN rather than through a redirect page.
Manual alignment remains enabled because an individual Sanborn sheet is not a modern orthographic GIS layer.

overlay.title: 1892 Key West Sanborn sheet 4 — experimental fit
overlay.attribution: Sanborn Map Company / Library of Congress, April 1892; public domain

periods.title: Key West immediately before the railway era
periods.summary: The April 1899 Sanborn edition contains 24 sheets and offers a detailed late-nineteenth-century record before the Overseas Extension and the Trumbo terminal landfill.
highlights: The displayed sheet records part of the developed city and can be compared with adjacent editions.
This is a useful baseline for separating nineteenth-century urban growth from twentieth-century rail and naval reclamation.
The full 24-sheet edition remains linked in the source catalog for block-level research.

overlay.title: 1899 Key West Sanborn sheet 4 — experimental fit
overlay.attribution: Sanborn Map Company / Library of Congress, April 1899; public domain

periods.title: Key West in the first railway year
periods.summary: The April 1912 Sanborn edition contains 33 sheets and was produced only months after the first train reached Key West. The displayed plate is delivered through the Library of Congress IIIF image service.
highlights: The Library of Congress dates the edition to April 1912, shortly after the January railway arrival.
Its expanded 33-sheet coverage can support detailed follow-on work around Trumbo Point, depots, wharves, and industrial parcels.
The displayed plate is an adjustable visual fit; waterfront plates should later be mosaicked and controlled separately.

overlay.title: 1912 Key West Sanborn plate 12 — experimental fit
overlay.attribution: Sanborn Map Company / Library of Congress, April 1912; public domain
